_comment: Design Constitution v1 — pass/fail rules for game design health. Each rule has a name, description, metric path (dotted, into metrics.json), comparator (one of <, >, <=, >=, ==, !=), threshold, and severity (PASS/FAIL or WARN). Validator runs each rule against the metrics and emits a validation report.

rules.name: no_dead_states
rules.description: Every movement state (FLOAT/PHASE/DIVE/COAST) should appear in at least 1%% of ticks. Dead states indicate an unreachable or useless mechanic.

rules.name: phase_used
rules.description: PHASE state should appear in at least 5%% of ticks. If lower, phase is underused — either too costly or not useful.

rules.name: dive_used
rules.description: DIVE state should appear in at least 1%% of ticks. If 0, the dive skill move is dead — either players don't know about it or it's not useful.

rules.name: coast_used
rules.description: COAST state should appear in at least 1%% of ticks. If 0, the coast state is dead — players cancel dive too early or don't carry momentum.

rules.name: no_dominant_state
rules.description: No single state should exceed 70%% of total time. Higher indicates a dominant strategy — the movement has degenerated to one move.

rules.name: min_chain_length
rules.description: Average chain length should be at least 2.0 states. Lower indicates one-move spam rather than expressive combo play.

rules.name: pulse_alive
rules.description: Pulse should fire at least 0.3 times per phase activation. Lower indicates pulse is dead — players don't use it in the chain.

rules.name: intentional_cancel_rate
rules.description: At least 30%% of phase exits should be manual cancels (intentional DIVE). Lower indicates players are letting phase expire naturally rather than engaging with the cancel skill move.

rules.name: expression_floor
rules.description: Expression score should be at least 30/100. Lower indicates the movement system is not being used expressively at all.

rules.name: momentum_not_dead
rules.description: Average momentum should be at least 0.3 (out of 2.0). Lower indicates momentum is not building — players aren't moving fast enough to engage the system.

rules.name: salvage_qte_pass_floor
rules.description: Salvage QTE pass rate should be at least 40%% (when QTEs occurred). Lower indicates QTEs are too hard or controls are unclear. Skip if no QTEs.

rules.name: salvage_qte_pass_ceiling
rules.description: Salvage QTE pass rate should be at most 90%% (when QTEs occurred). Higher indicates QTEs are too easy — no real risk.
